user_query:
I need a small command-line tool to help me reconcile device IDs from a partner’s BOM with human-readable names so I can brief our regional team.

Requirements:
1) Implement and run a script or CLI program that:
   - Reads the input file at input/devices.csv (columns: opportunity_id,vendor_id,device_id,units; vendor_id and device_id are hexadecimal, vendor_id may appear with or without the 0x prefix).
   - Downloads the canonical PCI ID database text file commonly named "pci.ids" from the official PCI ID Repository maintained by the linux-pci community. Do not hardcode a local copy; fetch it at runtime. Save the raw file under out/downloads/pci.ids and compute a SHA-256 hash.
   - Parses pci.ids to map vendor_id to vendor_name and (vendor_id + device_id) to device_name when available. Normalize vendor_id and device_id to lowercase 4-hex-digit strings without the 0x prefix before matching.
   - Enriches the input rows and writes out/resolved_devices.csv with columns:
     opportunity_id,vendor_id,vendor_name,device_id,device_name,units,resolution_status
     where resolution_status is one of: device_resolved (both names found), vendor_only (vendor found, device not), unresolved (neither found).
   - Aggregates to out/summary_by_vendor.csv with columns:
     vendor_id,vendor_name_or_UNKNOWN,total_units,resolved_device_rows,unresolved_device_rows
     (vendor_name_or_UNKNOWN should be the matched name or "UNKNOWN" if not found), sorted by total_units descending.
   - Writes a JSON log at out/logs/download.json capturing: source_description (e.g., "PCI ID Repository pci.ids"), fetched_datetime_iso8601, saved_path, sha256, first_two_noncomment_lines (as an array of strings), and vendors_parsed_count.
   - Produces a brief email draft at out/email_draft.txt to my regional sales team summarizing findings: include a subject line; mention total rows processed, total units, count of unresolved_device_rows, and list the top 2 vendors by total_units from out/summary_by_vendor.csv with their unit totals. Include the output paths (out/resolved_devices.csv and out/summary_by_vendor.csv) in the body so they can find the artifacts.

2) Run the tool against the provided input so all four artifacts exist after execution:
   - out/downloads/pci.ids
   - out/resolved_devices.csv
   - out/summary_by_vendor.csv
   - out/logs/download.json
   - out/email_draft.txt

Notes:
- The Internet download step is required; the tool should not rely on any bundled pci.ids.
- Use the downloaded pci.ids content only for name resolution; if a vendor or device is not present, set the corresponding name to "UNKNOWN" and the appropriate resolution_status.
- You may use any language, but provide a single runnable entry point that I can invoke from the workspace (for example: python3 scripts/resolve_gpu_ids.py --input input/devices.csv --outdir out) and ensure it produces the exact output paths above.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
